Title: wALADin Benzimidazoles Differentially Modulate the Function of Porphobilinogen Synthase Orthologs

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of 5-aminolevulinic acid to form porphobilinogen.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: wALADin1 inhibits PsPBGS activity and shifts the oligomeric equilibrium from active octamer toward low-activity hexamer, reducing porphobilinogen formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsPBGS was a core inhibited group X ortholog with wALADin1 IC50 of 10.6 ± 0.7 μM. 2. wALADin1 showed mixed competitive/noncompetitive inhibition and partial competition with Mg2+. 3. Native PAGE showed stabilization of inactive hexamers and reversal of the 5-ALA-induced octamer shift, explaining reduced porphobilinogen synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for heme and other tetrapyrroles essential for biological function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; IC50 profiling; kinetic analysis; nondenaturing PAGE with in-gel activity assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential herbicide target because photosynthetic eukaryote PBGS orthologs like PsPBGS are susceptible to inhibition by wALADins.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Inhibition of tetrapyrrole biosynthesis would likely impair essential plant metabolism and viability.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PsPBGS belongs to group X; 5-ALA and Mg2+ favor active octamer and oppose inhibitory morphlock action.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of 5-aminolevulinic acid to form porphobilinogen.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: wALADin1 and wALADin2 inhibit wPBGS by stabilizing inactive low-molecular-weight assemblies and reducing porphobilinogen formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. wPBGS is a group X ortholog strongly inhibited by wALADin1 and wALADin2. 2. Inhibition involves mixed competitive/noncompetitive behavior with functional competition against Mg2+ and 5-ALA-driven active-state formation. 3. Native PAGE reanalysis showed wALADin1 induces dimeric, tetrameric, and hexameric inactive assemblies, while wALADin2 mainly induces dimers.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for heme and other tetrapyrroles essential for biological function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; IC50 profiling; kinetic analysis; nondenaturing PAGE
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Validated anti-Wolbachia drug target; supports development of species-selective antimicrobial agents against filarial endosymbionts and related intracellular bacteria.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Essential-pathway targeting may select for compensatory metabolic responses; elevated 5-ALA could counter inhibition under some conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally thought not to alter oligomeric equilibrium, but revised experiments showed morphlock-like stabilization of inactive assemblies.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of 5-aminolevulinic acid to form porphobilinogen.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PaPBGS can be either stimulated or inhibited by wALADin1 depending on pH, 5-ALA, K+, and Mg2+; inhibition correlates with stabilization of inactive dimers, while stimulation is associated with active-octamer-favoring conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaPBGS displayed conditional dual behavior: stimulation under pH 8.0, high 5-ALA, and KCl, but inhibition under pH 7.5, low 5-ALA, and no KCl. 2. Under inhibitory conditions, wALADin1 functionally competed with allosteric Mg2+ and shifted protein toward inactive dimers. 3. K+ was required for stimulation and antagonized inhibition, while high protein concentration abolished the stimulatory effect, implying promotion of active oligomer assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for heme and other tetrapyrroles essential for biological function.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pH; 5-ALA concentration; KCl; Mg2+; protein concentration; wALADin1/wALADin2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; EC50/IC50 profiling; buffer scan; Mg2+ and substrate kinetics; thermal shift assay; nondenaturing PAGE
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antimicrobial target, but efficacy is highly condition-dependent; inhibitor design must account for oligomeric state and intracellular metabolite environment.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High substrate or octamer-promoting conditions can reverse inhibition into stimulation, potentially undermining antimicrobial efficacy.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although initially grouped with stimulated group Y orthologs, PaPBGS also exhibits group X-like inhibitory SAR under specific conditions.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of 5-aminolevulinic acid to form porphobilinogen.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: wALADin1 stimulates EcPBGS activity under assay conditions; stimulatory effect disappears at high protein concentration.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcPBGS is a group Y ortholog stimulated by wALADin1 and select derivatives. 2. Native PAGE did not show clear octamer stabilization, though high wALADin1 weakly induced low-molecular-weight species. 3. Loss of stimulation at high protein concentration suggests functional redundancy with octamer-promoting conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for heme and other tetrapyrroles essential for biological function.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: wALADin1 derivatives; Mg2+; Zn2+; protein concentration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; EC50 profiling; native PAGE
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited therapeutic utility as wALADins stimulate rather than inhibit this bacterial PBGS under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stimulation of tetrapyrrole precursor synthesis could be undesirable for antimicrobial deployment unless porphyrin overproduction proves toxic.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EcPBGS is ZnB-requiring catalytically, unlike several other group Y orthologs.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of 5-aminolevulinic acid to form porphobilinogen.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: wALADin1 stimulates VcPBGS activity under assay conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VcPBGS is a group Y ortholog stimulated by wALADin1 and selected derivatives. 2. Native PAGE showed no marked effect of wALADin1 on oligomeric equilibrium. 3. Stimulatory behavior was diminished at high protein concentration, consistent with modulation of active-assembly distribution rather than catalytic turnover of individual octamers.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for heme and other tetrapyrroles essential for biological function.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: wALADin1 derivatives; Mg2+; KCl; protein concentration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; EC50 profiling; native PAGE
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not an immediately favorable inhibitory antimicrobial target for this chemotype.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential stimulation of heme precursor production may complicate therapeutic use.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Weak inhibition by compounds 12 and 13 was noted only at high concentrations.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of 5-aminolevulinic acid to form porphobilinogen.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: wALADin1 stimulates YePBGS activity under assay conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YePBGS is a group Y ortholog activated by wALADin1 and several related derivatives. 2. Native PAGE showed no marked shift in oligomeric equilibrium upon wALADin1 treatment. 3. Reduced stimulation at high protein concentration suggests the compound promotes active assembly distribution rather than intrinsic catalytic rate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for heme and other tetrapyrroles essential for biological function.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: wALADin1 derivatives; Mg2+; KCl; protein concentration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; EC50 profiling; native PAGE
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not a favorable inhibitory target for this chemotype under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Compound-mediated stimulation could oppose antimicrobial aims.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No inhibitory conditions were found in buffer scans unlike PaPBGS.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of 5-aminolevulinic acid to form porphobilinogen.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: wALADin1 stimulates TgPBGS rather than inhibiting it under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TgPBGS belongs to stimulated group Y despite plastid origin. 2. Its response contrasts with chloroplast-derived PsPBGS and may relate to a C-terminal extension that stabilizes the octamer and prevents sampling of inhibitory hexameric assembly. 3. The stimulatory effect places TgPBGS outside the inhibitory chemotype behavior of group X plastid-like PBGS.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for heme and other tetrapyrroles essential for biological function.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Porphobilinogen synthase family with C-terminal extension stabilizing octamer
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Current wALADin chemotype is unlikely to serve as an inhibitor of TgPBGS; highlights need for ortholog-tailored modulators.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stimulation may complicate antiparasitic application unless increased porphyrin flux is toxic.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly notes plastid-derived TgPBGS behaves unlike pea PBGS.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of 5-aminolevulinic acid to form porphobilinogen.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DmPBGS is largely insensitive to wALADin1, with only weak inhibition by selected derivatives at high concentrations.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DmPBGS is a group Z ortholog insensitive to wALADin1. 2. Weak inhibition was observed with compounds 2, 3, 4, 8, 11, and 12, indicating a distinct SAR from group X and Y enzymes. 3. Lack of allosteric Mg2+ responsiveness correlates with insensitivity to wALADin1.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for heme and other tetrapyrroles essential for biological function.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports host selectivity because metazoan PBGS orthologs are largely insensitive to the inhibitory chemotype.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Zn2+-dependent metazoan ortholog lacking allosteric Mg2+-binding site.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of 5-aminolevulinic acid to form porphobilinogen.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HsPBGS is insensitive to wALADin1, with only weak inhibition by some derivatives reported from prior work and referenced here.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HsPBGS is a group Z ortholog insensitive to wALADin1. 2. Its SAR resembles DmPBGS and differs sharply from inhibitory group X and stimulatory group Y orthologs. 3. Lack of allosteric Mg2+ site may underlie insensitivity and host selectivity of wALADins.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for heme essential for cellular respiration and other hemoprotein functions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: referenced IC50 profiling from prior study integrated into comparative analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Favorable selectivity benchmark for development of antimicrobial or herbicidal PBGS modulators; authors also discuss future possibility of designing stimulators for human PBGS disorders.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Human PBGS stimulation, not inhibition, would be needed therapeutically in deficiency states.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Data for HsPBGS in this paper are drawn partly from previous reports.
